name: azure-devops
description: MCP server for interacting with Azure DevOps — manage projects, repositories, pull requests, work items, pipelines, wikis, test plans, and more directly from your AI assistant.
long_description:
The Azure DevOps MCP server brings the full power of Azure DevOps directly into AI-powered editors and agents. It provides 60+ tools organized across 9 functional domains:

- **Core**: List projects, teams, and search identities
- **Repositories**: Manage repos, branches, pull requests, commits, and file browsing
- **Work Items**: Create, update, query, and link work items with full backlog support
- **Pipelines**: Manage builds, pipeline definitions, runs, logs, and artifacts
- **Wiki**: Browse, read, and edit wiki pages
- **Test Plans**: Create and manage test plans, suites, and test cases
- **Search**: Search code, wiki pages, and work items
- **Work**: Manage iterations, team assignments, and capacities
- **Advanced Security**: View repository security alerts

Supports multiple authentication methods including interactive OAuth, Azure CLI, environment variables, and token-based authentication. Domains can be selectively enabled to reduce tool count for optimal AI model performance.

domains.title: Enabled Domains
domains.description: Space-separated list of domains to enable (e.g. 'core repositories work-items'). Leave empty for all domains.

authentication.title: Authentication Method
authentication.description: Authentication method: 'interactive' (OAuth via browser), 'azcli' (Azure CLI), 'env' (Azure SDK DefaultAzureCredential), or 'envvar' (ADO_MCP_AUTH_TOKEN environment variable)

tenant.title: Azure Tenant ID
tenant.description: Optional Azure tenant ID for multi-tenant scenarios

auth_token.title: Authentication Token
auth_token.description: Azure DevOps personal access token (required when authentication is set to 'envvar')
